quietHours.notes: Los Angeles's noise code is built around an ambient-plus-offset standard rather than a single flat quiet-hours rule — see decibelLimits.measurementNotes. TODO_VERIFY: whether a distinct weekend quiet-hours window exists separate from the weekday one.

decibelLimits.measurementNotes: LAMC does not set one flat residential number. Instead, under LAMC Sec. 111.03 and related sections, sound from a source generally may not exceed the presumed ambient noise level by more than 5 dBA on adjacent residential property, with a lower presumed ambient at night than during the day. TODO_VERIFY: the exact presumed-ambient dBA figures and the offset rule, confirmed directly against the LAMC text rather than secondary summaries, before quoting a specific number.

ordinance.name: Los Angeles Municipal Code — Noise Regulation (Chapter XI)

enforcement.secondary: Los Angeles Police Department non-emergency line
enforcement.howToReport: Call LAPD's non-emergency line for an in-progress disturbance; chronic issues can be reported to the LAPD Noise Enforcement Team.

sources.label: LAMC Sec. 111.03 — Minimum ambient noise level
